---

## Known Issues / Tech Debt

- **Section collapse animation** — `{open && ...}` conditional rendering means `max-h` transition snaps instead of animates. Fix: always render cards, toggle only `max-h` + `opacity`. (Promoted to Up Next above.)
- **Archive page reads full JSON per issue** — loads all newsletter JSON files at request time to get titles. Fine now, worth replacing with a lightweight `data/index.json` (date → title → storyCount) once the archive grows past ~100 issues.
- **`date_override` workflow input is unused** — the `generate-newsletter.yml` defines a `date_override` manual dispatch input but never passes it to the script as an env var. The feature is silently broken.
- **Category mismatch** — `CATEGORY_STYLES` in `newsletter-types.ts` defines `Industry` and `Startups` badges but the Claude prompt doesn't include them as valid categories, so they're dead code. Either add to prompt or remove from styles.

- **Removed** 5 GitHub `.atom` feeds — no more Ghostty commit noise
- **Added** Emad Mostaque, Nathan Benaich (practitioners)
- **Added** Ben's Bites, SemiAnalysis, Weights & Biases, Allen AI, Roboflow, IBM Research (broader sources)

Always commit and push to `nfc-eng/claude-config` after making any changes to `~/.claude/` — including `settings.json`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.gitignore`, or any other tracked file.

file.content:
name: Generate AI Newsletter

on:
  schedule:
    # 6 AM Eastern Standard Time (UTC-5) = 11:00 UTC
    # Note: shifts to 7 AM Eastern during summer (EDT, UTC-4)
    - cron: '0 11 * * *'

  # Allow manual trigger from the GitHub Actions tab
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      date_override:
        description: 'Override date (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave blank for today.'
        required: false
        default: ''

jobs:
  generate:
    name: Generate Newsletter
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 45
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Generate newsletter
        timeout-minutes: 35
        run: node scripts/generate-newsletter.js
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

      - name: Commit and push newsletter
        id: commit
        timeout-minutes: 5
        run: |
          git config user.name "nfc-eng"
          git config user.email "58239972+nfc-eng@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Stage only the data directory
          git add data/

          # Only commit if there are changes
          if git diff --staged --quiet; then
            echo "No changes to commit (newsletter for today already exists)"
            echo "pushed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            TODAY=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
            git commit -m "Newsletter: ${TODAY}"
            git push origin HEAD
            echo "Newsletter committed and pushed for ${TODAY}"
            echo "pushed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Trigger Vercel deploy
        if: steps.commit.outputs.pushed == 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 2
        run: |
          curl -s --max-time 60 -X POST "https://api.vercel.com/v1/integrations/deploy/prj_1hmwUaJ1XIKqaCIK8t3G7Dbh1lfS/dKLuRFPKSN"
          echo "Vercel deploy hook triggered"

content.text:
Two issues:
1. The `schedule` trigger always runs on the repo's **default branch** — which based on what we saw earlier is `claude/ai-newsletter-automation-DIJzS`, not `mainline`
2. The checkout step has no `ref:` specified, so it follows the default branch

Fix: pin the checkout to `mainline` explicitly.

        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
input.new_string:
- name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: mainline
          fetch-depth: 1

name: Generate AI Newsletter

on:
  schedule:
    # 6 AM Eastern Standard Time (UTC-5) = 11:00 UTC
    # Note: shifts to 7 AM Eastern during summer (EDT, UTC-4)
    - cron: '0 11 * * *'

  # Allow manual trigger from the GitHub Actions tab
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      date_override:
        description: 'Override date (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave blank for today.'
        required: false
        default: ''

jobs:
  generate:
    name: Generate Newsletter
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 45
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Generate newsletter
        timeout-minutes: 35
        run: node scripts/generate-newsletter.js
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

      - name: Commit and push newsletter
        id: commit
        timeout-minutes: 5
        run: |
          git config user.name "nfc-eng"
          git config user.email "58239972+nfc-eng@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Stage only the data directory
          git add data/

          # Only commit if there are changes
          if git diff --staged --quiet; then
            echo "No changes to commit (newsletter for today already exists)"
            echo "pushed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            TODAY=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
            git commit -m "Newsletter: ${TODAY}"
            git push origin HEAD
            echo "Newsletter committed and pushed for ${TODAY}"
            echo "pushed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Trigger Vercel deploy
        if: steps.commit.outputs.pushed == 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 2
        run: |
          curl -s --max-time 60 -X POST "https://api.vercel.com/v1/integrations/deploy/prj_1hmwUaJ1XIKqaCIK8t3G7Dbh1lfS/dKLuRFPKSN"
          echo "Vercel deploy hook triggered"

message.content: Now the next thing I want to do is take this and turn it into a podcast that users can click like a media button at the top of the website and listen to the update for the day in like a podcast form. I'd love for this podcast to sound as natural as possible. How can we try to do that? Please update roadmap and commit and will first talk with me about the plan and the best way to implement this and then I want us to reflect it in the roadmap so that we have a plan and then we can shift gears and actually do it.

content.text:
Great feature idea. Let me lay out the full picture before we touch anything.

---

## The Plan

### Step 1 — Podcast Script Generation (Claude)
The newsletter JSON is structured data, not natural speech. Before hitting TTS, we use Claude to rewrite it as a podcast script — conversational, with transitions between stories, a host persona, natural spoken cadence. This is the difference between a robot reading bullet points and something that actually sounds like a show.

### Step 2 — Text-to-Speech
Two realistic options:

| | **OpenAI TTS** | **ElevenLabs** |
|---|---|---|
| Quality | Very good | Best in class |
| Cost | ~$0.05/day (tts-1-hd) | Free tier 10K chars/mo, then $5/mo |
| Naturalness | Good, slight AI-ish cadence | Nearly indistinguishable from human |
| Setup | Simple API call | Simple API call |

**My recommendation: ElevenLabs.** The naturalness gap is significant and their free tier probably covers daily usage. If the newsletter script is ~2,500 words (~15K chars/month), you'd be just over free — $5/mo.

### Step 3 — Audio Hosting
Can't commit MP3s to git. Options:
- **Vercel Blob** — already on Vercel, simplest integration, generous free tier
- **Cloudflare R2** — free for low traffic, slightly more setup

**Recommendation: Vercel Blob.** One less service to manage.

### Step 4 — Pipeline Integration
Add two steps to the existing GitHub Actions workflow after newsletter generation:
1. Claude generates podcast script from newsletter JSON
2. ElevenLabs converts script → MP3, uploads to Vercel Blob
3. Audio URL gets saved into the newsletter JSON

### Step 5 — Player UI
A sticky play bar at the top of the newsletter page — play/pause, scrubber, time. Clean, minimal. Reads the `audioUrl` field from the newsletter JSON.

---

## Open Questions Before We Start
1. **One voice or two?** Single host is simpler. A host + co-host dialogue sounds more like a real podcast but requires two TTS passes and a script with conversation structure.
2. **Length target?** A 10-story newsletter at natural pace is ~8-12 minutes. Good with that?
3. **Do you have an ElevenLabs account?** Or should we design for OpenAI TTS first?

---

Sound good? I'll update the roadmap with this plan and then we can start building.

message.content: I would lprefer one host, yeah 8-12 minutes is perfect, maybe deep dive one article? the most impactful or interesting article of the day??

content.text:
Love it. The deep dive is the killer feature — every other AI newsletter just does headlines. A 2-3 minute "here's why this one actually matters" section on the day's biggest story is genuinely differentiated.

**Script structure:**
1. **Cold open** — one punchy sentence teasing the deep dive topic
2. **Headlines** — quick hits on each story, ~20-30 seconds each
3. **Deep Dive** — Claude picks the most significant story, 2-3 minutes of context, implications, and what builders should actually do with it
4. **Outro** — 15 seconds, see you tomorrow

Let me get that in the roadmap now.

content.content:

# AI Daily Flash — Roadmap

Items are roughly ordered by impact vs. effort. Not a strict backlog — more a living list of what's worth doing next.

---

## In Progress / Recently Shipped

- [x] **Cross-day duplicate detection** — Before generating, load the last 7 days of newsletters and strip any URL already published. Also surfaces the recent issue list to Claude in the prompt so it has narrative context. Recent issues now appear as navigation links at the bottom of every newsletter page.
- [x] **Practitioner feed network** — 30+ practitioner feeds (Karpathy, Willison, Huyen, Lambert, etc.) weighted higher than broadcast sources. GitHub activity feeds included per practitioner.
- [x] **Per-author story cap** — Max 2 stories per source per issue to avoid any single feed dominating.
- [x] **Collapsible sections** — Newsletter sections are expand/collapse with animated chevron.

---

## Up Next

### Fix Section Collapse Animation (Tech Debt)
The current `{open && section.stories.map(...)}` pattern unmounts cards when closed, so the `max-h` CSS transition has nothing to animate — sections snap open/closed instead of sliding. Fix: always render cards, gate visibility with `max-h` + `opacity` only.

### Prev / Next Navigation on Dated Pages
- Add explicit `← older` / `newer →` arrows on every dated issue page
- Currently only the header has a "Previous issue" link; no way to page forward if you're reading an old issue

### Archive Page Polish
- Show story count per issue (`12 stories · 4 sections`)
- Add issue number (e.g. `#14`) derived from position in the archive
- Show formatted date alongside the ISO date string

### Issue Numbers

file.content:
### Issue Numbers
- Assign a stable `#N` issue number to each newsletter at generation time, stored in the JSON
- Display in hero, archive, and page title (`Issue #14 — March 12, 2026`)

### Story Count in Section Headers
- Show `(4)` next to each section title so readers know what's inside before expanding

### "Why This Matters" on Every Story
Inspired by Import AI's format — the single most replicable high-signal pattern across top competitors. Every story summary should end with a concrete "why it matters to someone building with AI" sentence. This is distinct from the summary itself.

**What to do:**
- Add a `whyItMatters` field to the `Story` schema in `newsletter-types.ts`
- Update the Claude prompt to generate a 1-sentence `whyItMatters` per story
- Display it as a subtle callout below the summary in `StoryCard` (e.g. small cyan label + italic text)

---

## Better GitHub Feed Fetching
GitHub Atom feeds (`github.com/{user}.atom`) currently expose nothing useful — a commit message like "fix typo" or "update deps" from a practitioner tells the reader nothing. The feed entry has no context about *what changed*, *why it matters*, or *what project it's in*.

**What to do:**
- For each GitHub atom item, parse out the repo name and commit SHA from the link
- Use the GitHub REST API (`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{sha}`) to fetch the full commit message, changed files, and optionally the diff stat
- For release events (`/releases`), fetch the release notes
- For new repos created by practitioners, fetch the repo description and README intro
- Filter out noise (typo fixes, dep bumps, CI config changes) and only surface items where the diff involves meaningful code or the commit message signals intent
- Weight these as first-class stories when a practitioner ships something new
- Requires adding a `GITHUB_TOKEN` secret to the Actions workflow

This makes practitioner GitHub activity as rich as a blog post rather than a cryptic one-liner.

file.content:
---

## Bigger Ideas

### Category / Tag Filtering
- Sticky filter bar on the newsletter page to show only stories in a chosen category (Models, Research, Tools, etc.) across all sections
- Extend to the archive: `/archive?category=Research` shows all issues containing Research stories
- Competitor gap: The Batch has partial tag pages (`/tag/research/`) but no true faceted filtering — this would be best-in-class

### Per-Category RSS Feeds
- Expose granular RSS endpoints: `/feed/models.xml`, `/feed/research.xml`, `/feed/open-source.xml` etc.
- No competitor currently offers this — developers who want only paper announcements or only open-source drops could subscribe to a focused feed
- Low implementation effort given we already have category data in every story

### Reading Time Estimate
- Calculate and display estimated reading time in the hero (`~6 min read`)
- Can be computed at render time from total summary word count

### Search
- Full-text search across all past newsletters, powered by a simple JSON index generated at build time
- Semantic search (AI-powered) would be a meaningful differentiator — no competitor has it
- Start with basic full-text, upgrade to vector search later

### "As Covered In" Back-References
- When a story topic resurfaces across days, surface a subtle link: `Also covered: March 10 →`
- Requires semantic similarity matching, not just URL dedup

### Thumbs Up / Thumbs Down
- Hide consistently bad posts, surface good ones
- User feedback loop to improve curation over time

### Save for Later
- When users create accounts, allow saving favorite articles for later review or download

### Email Delivery
- Send the newsletter to a subscriber list via Resend or similar on generation
- Store subscriber emails outside the repo (environment variable or database)
- All top competitors have email as their primary channel — the web-only approach is a long-term ceiling on reach

### Trending GitHub Repos Section
- Inspired by AlphaSignal — a dedicated section per issue for the most-starred/trending open-source repos in AI from the past 24–48h
- Pull from GitHub Trending or the GitHub REST API search endpoint sorted by `stars` + `pushed`
- Label repos with language, star count, and a one-line description
- Highly developer-native signal that no current news source does well in a newsletter format

### Dark Mode
- The site is already dark-by-default (zinc-950 base). This item is about respecting `prefers-color-scheme: light` for users who prefer light mode.
- Implement using Tailwind's `dark:` classes with a `class` strategy toggle on `<html>`

### Annual Reading List / "Best Of"
- A curated `/reading-list` page: the best practitioner posts, papers, and tools from the past year
- Latent Space's annual reading list is a perennial resource that drives SEO and return visits long after it's published
- Can be hand-curated or auto-generated from the highest-engagement stories across issues
---

## Known Issues / Tech Debt

- **Section collapse animation** — `{open && ...}` conditional rendering means `max-h` transition snaps instead of animates. Fix: always render cards, toggle only `max-h` + `opacity`. (Promoted to Up Next above.)
- **Archive page reads full JSON per issue** — loads all newsletter JSON files at request time to get titles. Fine now, worth replacing with a lightweight `data/index.json` (date → title → storyCount) once the archive grows past ~100 issues.
- **`date_override` workflow input is unused** — the `generate-newsletter.yml` defines a `date_override` manual dispatch input but never passes it to the script as an env var. The feature is silently broken.
- **Category mismatch** — `CATEGORY_STYLES` in `newsletter-types.ts` defines `Industry` and `Startups` badges but the Claude prompt doesn't include them as valid categories, so they're dead code. Either add to prompt or remove from styles.

content.text: Now I'll add the podcast feature as the top priority in "Up Next":

input.old_string:
## Up Next

### Fix Section Collapse Animation (Tech Debt)
input.new_string:
## Up Next

### 🎙️ Daily Podcast — Audio Edition of Every Issue

A play button in the newsletter hero lets users listen to the day's issue as a natural-sounding ~10 minute podcast. Every competitor is read-only — this is a meaningful differentiator.

**Script structure (generated by Claude from the newsletter JSON):**
1. **Cold open** — one punchy sentence teasing the deep dive topic (~10 sec)
2. **Headlines** — quick hits on each story, ~20-30 seconds each
3. **Deep Dive** — Claude selects the single most impactful story of the day and delivers 2-3 minutes of context, implications, and what builders should actually do with it
4. **Outro** — 15 seconds, see you tomorrow

**Implementation plan:**

**Step 1 — Podcast script generation (Claude)**
- After the newsletter JSON is saved, make a second Claude call to generate a podcast script
- Prompt instructs Claude to write for the ear, not the eye: no bullet points, natural transitions, conversational cadence, single host voice
- Claude also selects and deeply covers the most impactful story of the day for the deep dive segment
- Script saved to `data/scripts/YYYY-MM-DD.txt` for debugging/review

**Step 2 — Text-to-speech (ElevenLabs)**
- Feed the script to ElevenLabs API (single voice, highest quality model)
- ElevenLabs chosen over OpenAI TTS for naturalness — nearly indistinguishable from human at this length
- Free tier covers ~10K chars/month; paid is $5/mo for 30K chars (well within budget)
- Output: MP3, ~8-12 minutes
- Requires `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` secret in GitHub Actions

**Step 3 — Audio hosting (Vercel Blob)**
- MP3 uploaded to Vercel Blob storage after generation
- Public URL saved as `audioUrl` field in the newsletter JSON
- Vercel Blob chosen: already on Vercel, generous free tier, zero extra infrastructure

**Step 4 — Player UI**
- Sticky mini-player or inline hero play button on each newsletter page
- Reads `audioUrl` from newsletter JSON — shows nothing if audio hasn't generated yet (graceful fallback)
- Controls: play/pause, scrubber, current time / total duration
- Styled to match the dark zinc aesthetic

**Step 5 — GitHub Actions integration**
Add two steps to `generate-newsletter.yml` after the existing newsletter commit:
1. Run script generation + ElevenLabs TTS
2. Upload MP3 to Vercel Blob, patch `audioUrl` into the newsletter JSON, commit updated JSON

**Secrets needed:**
- `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` — ElevenLabs account
- `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` — Vercel Blob token

---

### Fix Section Collapse Animation (Tech Debt)

# AI Daily Flash — Roadmap

Items are roughly ordered by impact vs. effort. Not a strict backlog — more a living list of what's worth doing next.

---

## In Progress / Recently Shipped

- [x] **Cross-day duplicate detection** — Before generating, load the last 7 days of newsletters and strip any URL already published. Also surfaces the recent issue list to Claude in the prompt so it has narrative context. Recent issues now appear as navigation links at the bottom of every newsletter page.
- [x] **Practitioner feed network** — 30+ practitioner feeds (Karpathy, Willison, Huyen, Lambert, etc.) weighted higher than broadcast sources. GitHub activity feeds included per practitioner.
- [x] **Per-author story cap** — Max 2 stories per source per issue to avoid any single feed dominating.
- [x] **Collapsible sections** — Newsletter sections are expand/collapse with animated chevron.

---

## Up Next

### Fix Section Collapse Animation (Tech Debt)
The current `{open && section.stories.map(...)}` pattern unmounts cards when closed, so the `max-h` CSS transition has nothing to animate — sections snap open/closed instead of sliding. Fix: always render cards, gate visibility with `max-h` + `opacity` only.

### Prev / Next Navigation on Dated Pages
- Add explicit `← older` / `newer →` arrows on every dated issue page
- Currently only the header has a "Previous issue" link; no way to page forward if you're reading an old issue

### Archive Page Polish
- Show story count per issue (`12 stories · 4 sections`)
- Add issue number (e.g. `#14`) derived from position in the archive
- Show formatted date alongside the ISO date string

### Issue Numbers
- Assign a stable `#N` issue number to each newsletter at generation time, stored in the JSON
- Display in hero, archive, and page title (`Issue #14 — March 12, 2026`)

### Story Count in Section Headers
- Show `(4)` next to each section title so readers know what's inside before expanding

### "Why This Matters" on Every Story
Inspired by Import AI's format — the single most replicable high-signal pattern across top competitors. Every story summary should end with a concrete "why it matters to someone building with AI" sentence. This is distinct from the summary itself.

**What to do:**
- Add a `whyItMatters` field to the `Story` schema in `newsletter-types.ts`
- Update the Claude prompt to generate a 1-sentence `whyItMatters` per story
- Display it as a subtle callout below the summary in `StoryCard` (e.g. small cyan label + italic text)

---

## Better GitHub Feed Fetching
GitHub Atom feeds (`github.com/{user}.atom`) currently expose nothing useful — a commit message like "fix typo" or "update deps" from a practitioner tells the reader nothing. The feed entry has no context about *what changed*, *why it matters*, or *what project it's in*.

**What to do:**
- For each GitHub atom item, parse out the repo name and commit SHA from the link
- Use the GitHub REST API (`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{sha}`) to fetch the full commit message, changed files, and optionally the diff stat

- Filter out noise (typo fixes, dep bumps, CI config changes) and only surface items where the diff involves meaningful code or the commit message signals intent
- Weight these as first-class stories when a practitioner ships something new
- Requires adding a `GITHUB_TOKEN` secret to the Actions workflow

This makes practitioner GitHub activity as rich as a blog post rather than a cryptic one-liner.

---

## Bigger Ideas

### Category / Tag Filtering

- Extend to the archive: `/archive?category=Research` shows all issues containing Research stories

### Per-Category RSS Feeds

- No competitor currently offers this — developers who want only paper announcements or only open-source drops could subscribe to a focused feed

### Reading Time Estimate

### Search

### Thumbs Up / Thumbs Down

### Save for Later

### Email Delivery
- Send the newsletter to a subscriber list via Resend or similar on generation

- All top competitors have email as their primary channel — the web-only approach is a long-term ceiling on reach

### Trending GitHub Repos Section

- **Archive page reads full JSON per issue** — loads all newsletter JSON files at request time to get titles. Fine now, worth replacing with a lightweight `data/index.json` (date → title → storyCount) once the archive grows past ~100 issues.

lines: +### 🎙️ Daily Podcast — Audio Edition of Every Issue
+A play button in the newsletter hero lets users listen to the day's issue as a natural-sounding ~10 minute podcast. Every competitor is read-only — this is a meaningful differentiator.
+**Script structure (generated by Claude from the newsletter JSON):**
+1. **Cold open** — one punchy sentence teasing the deep dive topic (~10 sec)
+2. **Headlines** — quick hits on each story, ~20-30 seconds each
+3. **Deep Dive** — Claude selects the single most impactful story of the day and delivers 2-3 minutes of context, implications, and what builders should actually do with it
+- After the newsletter JSON is saved, make a second Claude call to generate a podcast script
+- Prompt instructs Claude to write for the ear, not the eye: no bullet points, natural transitions, conversational cadence, single host voice
+- Claude also selects and deeply covers the most impactful story of the day for the deep dive segment
+- Feed the script to ElevenLabs API (single voice, highest quality model)
+- ElevenLabs chosen over OpenAI TTS for naturalness — nearly indistinguishable from human at this length
+- Free tier covers ~10K chars/month; paid is $5/mo for 30K chars (well within budget)
+- MP3 uploaded to Vercel Blob storage after generation
+- Public URL saved as `audioUrl` field in the newsletter JSON
+- Vercel Blob chosen: already on Vercel, generous free tier, zero extra infrastructure
+- Sticky mini-player or inline hero play button on each newsletter page
+- Reads `audioUrl` from newsletter JSON — shows nothing if audio hasn't generated yet (graceful fallback)
+- Controls: play/pause, scrubber, current time / total duration
+- Styled to match the dark zinc aesthetic
+Add two steps to `generate-newsletter.yml` after the existing newsletter commit:
+2. Upload MP3 to Vercel Blob, patch `audioUrl` into the newsletter JSON, commit updated JSON

The current `{open && section.stories.map(...)}` pattern unmounts cards when closed, so the `max-h` CSS transition has nothing to animate — sections snap open/closed instead of sliding. Fix: always render cards, gate visibility with `max-h` + `opacity` only.

## Known Issues / Tech Debt

### Bump GitHub Actions Node.js versions (by June 2, 2026)
- `actions/checkout@v4` and `actions/setup-node@v4` are running on Node.js 20 which GitHub is deprecating on June 2, 2026

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# AI Daily Flash — Roadmap

Items are roughly ordered by impact vs. effort. Not a strict backlog — more a living list of what's worth doing next.

---

## In Progress / Recently Shipped

- [x] **Cross-day duplicate detection** — Before generating, load the last 7 days of newsletters and strip any URL already published. Also surfaces the recent issue list to Claude in the prompt so it has narrative context. Recent issues now appear as navigation links at the bottom of every newsletter page.
- [x] **Practitioner feed network** — 30+ practitioner feeds (Karpathy, Willison, Huyen, Lambert, etc.) weighted higher than broadcast sources. GitHub activity feeds included per practitioner.
- [x] **Per-author story cap** — Max 2 stories per source per issue to avoid any single feed dominating.
- [x] **Collapsible sections** — Newsletter sections are expand/collapse with animated chevron.

---

## Up Next

### 🎙️ Daily Podcast — Audio Edition of Every Issue

A play button in the newsletter hero lets users listen to the day's issue as a natural-sounding ~10 minute podcast. Every competitor is read-only — this is a meaningful differentiator.

**Script structure (generated by Claude from the newsletter JSON):**
1. **Cold open** — one punchy sentence teasing the deep dive topic (~10 sec)
2. **Headlines** — quick hits on each story, ~20-30 seconds each
3. **Deep Dive** — Claude selects the single most impactful story of the day and delivers 2-3 minutes of context, implications, and what builders should actually do with it
4. **Outro** — 15 seconds, see you tomorrow

**Implementation plan:**

**Step 1 — Podcast script generation (Claude)**
- After the newsletter JSON is saved, make a second Claude call to generate a podcast script
- Prompt instructs Claude to write for the ear, not the eye: no bullet points, natural transitions, conversational cadence, single host voice
- Claude also selects and deeply covers the most impactful story of the day for the deep dive segment
- Script saved to `data/scripts/YYYY-MM-DD.txt` for debugging/review

**Step 2 — Text-to-speech (ElevenLabs)**
- Feed the script to ElevenLabs API (single voice, highest quality model)
- ElevenLabs chosen over OpenAI TTS for naturalness — nearly indistinguishable from human at this length
- Free tier covers ~10K chars/month; paid is $5/mo for 30K chars (well within budget)
- Output: MP3, ~8-12 minutes
- Requires `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` secret in GitHub Actions

**Step 3 — Audio hosting (Vercel Blob)**
- MP3 uploaded to Vercel Blob storage after generation
- Public URL saved as `audioUrl` field in the newsletter JSON
- Vercel Blob chosen: already on Vercel, generous free tier, zero extra infrastructure

**Step 4 — Player UI**
- Sticky mini-player or inline hero play button on each newsletter page
- Reads `audioUrl` from newsletter JSON — shows nothing if audio hasn't generated yet (graceful fallback)
- Controls: play/pause, scrubber, current time / total duration
- Styled to match the dark zinc aesthetic

**Step 5 — GitHub Actions integration**
Add two steps to `generate-newsletter.yml` after the existing newsletter commit:
1. Run script generation + ElevenLabs TTS
2. Upload MP3 to Vercel Blob, patch `audioUrl` into the newsletter JSON, commit updated JSON

**Secrets needed:**
- `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` — ElevenLabs account
- `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` — Vercel Blob token

---

### Fix Section Collapse Animation (Tech Debt)
The current `{open && section.stories.map(...)}` pattern unmounts cards when closed, so the `max-h` CSS transition has nothing to animate — sections snap open/closed instead of sliding. Fix: always render cards, gate visibility with `max-h` + `opacity` only.

### Prev / Next Navigation on Dated Pages
- Add explicit `← older` / `newer →` arrows on every dated issue page
- Currently only the header has a "Previous issue" link; no way to page forward if you're reading an old issue

### Archive Page Polish
- Show story count per issue (`12 stories · 4 sections`)
- Add issue number (e.g. `#14`) derived from position in the archive
- Show formatted date alongside the ISO date string

### Issue Numbers
- Assign a stable `#N` issue number to each newsletter at generation time, stored in the JSON
- Display in hero, archive, and page title (`Issue #14 — March 12, 2026`)

### Story Count in Section Headers
- Show `(4)` next to each section title so readers know what's inside before expanding

### "Why This Matters" on Every Story
Inspired by Import AI's format — the single most replicable high-signal pattern across top competitors. Every story summary should end with a concrete "why it matters to someone building with AI" sentence. This is distinct from the summary itself.

**What to do:**
- Add a `whyItMatters` field to the `Story` schema in `newsletter-types.ts`
- Update the Claude prompt to generate a 1-sentence `whyItMatters` per story
- Display it as a subtle callout below the summary in `StoryCard` (e.g. small cyan label + italic text)

---

## Better GitHub Feed Fetching
GitHub Atom feeds (`github.com/{user}.atom`) currently expose nothing useful — a commit message like "fix typo" or "update deps" from a practitioner tells the reader nothing. The feed entry has no context about *what changed*, *why it matters*, or *what project it's in*.

**What to do:**
- For each GitHub atom item, parse out the repo name and commit SHA from the link
- Use the GitHub REST API (`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{sha}`) to fetch the full commit message, changed files, and optionally the diff stat
- For release events (`/releases`), fetch the release notes
- For new repos created by practitioners, fetch the repo description and README intro
- Filter out noise (typo fixes, dep bumps, CI config changes) and only surface items where the diff involves meaningful code or the commit message signals intent
- Weight these as first-class stories when a practitioner ships something new
- Requires adding a `GITHUB_TOKEN` secret to the Actions workflow

This makes practitioner GitHub activity as rich as a blog post rather than a cryptic one-liner.

---

## Bigger Ideas

### Category / Tag Filtering
- Sticky filter bar on the newsletter page to show only stories in a chosen category (Models, Research, Tools, etc.) across all sections
- Extend to the archive: `/archive?category=Research` shows all issues containing Research stories
- Competitor gap: The Batch has partial tag pages (`/tag/research/`) but no true faceted filtering — this would be best-in-class

### Per-Category RSS Feeds
- Expose granular RSS endpoints: `/feed/models.xml`, `/feed/research.xml`, `/feed/open-source.xml` etc.
- No competitor currently offers this — developers who want only paper announcements or only open-source drops could subscribe to a focused feed
- Low implementation effort given we already have category data in every story

### Reading Time Estimate
- Calculate and display estimated reading time in the hero (`~6 min read`)
- Can be computed at render time from total summary word count

### Search
- Full-text search across all past newsletters, powered by a simple JSON index generated at build time
- Semantic search (AI-powered) would be a meaningful differentiator — no competitor has it
- Start with basic full-text, upgrade to vector search later

### "As Covered In" Back-References
- When a story topic resurfaces across days, surface a subtle link: `Also covered: March 10 →`
- Requires semantic similarity matching, not just URL dedup

### Thumbs Up / Thumbs Down
- Hide consistently bad posts, surface good ones
- User feedback loop to improve curation over time

### Save for Later
- When users create accounts, allow saving favorite articles for later review or download

### Email Delivery
- Send the newsletter to a subscriber list via Resend or similar on generation
- Store subscriber emails outside the repo (environment variable or database)
- All top competitors have email as their primary channel — the web-only approach is a long-term ceiling on reach

### Trending GitHub Repos Section
- Inspired by AlphaSignal — a dedicated section per issue for the most-starred/trending open-source repos in AI from the past 24–48h
- Pull from GitHub Trending or the GitHub REST API search endpoint sorted by `stars` + `pushed`
- Label repos with language, star count, and a one-line description
- Highly developer-native signal that no current news source does well in a newsletter format

### Dark Mode
- The site is already dark-by-default (zinc-950 base). This item is about respecting `prefers-color-scheme: light` for users who prefer light mode.
- Implement using Tailwind's `dark:` classes with a `class` strategy toggle on `<html>`

### Annual Reading List / "Best Of"
- A curated `/reading-list` page: the best practitioner posts, papers, and tools from the past year
- Latent Space's annual reading list is a perennial resource that drives SEO and return visits long after it's published
- Can be hand-curated or auto-generated from the highest-engagement stories across issues
---

## Known Issues / Tech Debt

- **Section collapse animation** — `{open && ...}` conditional rendering means `max-h` transition snaps instead of animates. Fix: always render cards, toggle only `max-h` + `opacity`. (Promoted to Up Next above.)
- **Archive page reads full JSON per issue** — loads all newsletter JSON files at request time to get titles. Fine now, worth replacing with a lightweight `data/index.json` (date → title → storyCount) once the archive grows past ~100 issues.
- **`date_override` workflow input is unused** — the `generate-newsletter.yml` defines a `date_override` manual dispatch input but never passes it to the script as an env var. The feature is silently broken.
- **Category mismatch** — `CATEGORY_STYLES` in `newsletter-types.ts` defines `Industry` and `Startups` badges but the Claude prompt doesn't include them as valid categories, so they're dead code. Either add to prompt or remove from styles.

lines: +### Bump GitHub Actions Node.js versions (by June 2, 2026)
+- `actions/checkout@v4` and `actions/setup-node@v4` are running on Node.js 20 which GitHub is deprecating on June 2, 2026
+- Fix: update both to their latest versions that support Node.js 24, or set `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` in the workflow

- **Section collapse animation** — `{open && ...}` conditional rendering means `max-h` transition snaps instead of animates. Fix: always render cards, toggle only `max-h` + `opacity`. (Promoted to Up Next above.)

- **Archive page reads full JSON per issue** — loads all newsletter JSON files at request time to get titles. Fine now, worth replacing with a lightweight `data/index.json` (date → title → storyCount) once the archive grows past ~100 issues.

- **`date_override` workflow input is unused** — the `generate-newsletter.yml` defines a `date_override` manual dispatch input but never passes it to the script as an env var. The feature is silently broken.

content.text:
Here's what you need to do on Vercel:

1. Go to your **Vercel dashboard** → click **Storage** in the left sidebar
2. Click **Create Database** → select **Blob**
3. Name it anything (e.g. `ai-daily-flash-audio`) → click Create
4. Once created, go to the **Settings** tab of the blob store → copy the **`BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN`**
5. Add it as a GitHub repo secret: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New secret → `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN`**

Do that and let me know when it's done. While you're setting that up I'll write the podcast generation code so it's ready to wire in the moment you have the token.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Generate AI Newsletter

on:
  schedule:
    # 6 AM Eastern Standard Time (UTC-5) = 11:00 UTC
    # Note: shifts to 7 AM Eastern during summer (EDT, UTC-4)
    - cron: '0 11 * * *'

  # Allow manual trigger from the GitHub Actions tab
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      date_override:
        description: 'Override date (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave blank for today.'
        required: false
        default: ''

jobs:
  generate:
    name: Generate Newsletter
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 45
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: mainline
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Generate newsletter
        timeout-minutes: 35
        run: node scripts/generate-newsletter.js
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

      - name: Commit and push newsletter
        id: commit
        timeout-minutes: 5
        run: |
          git config user.name "nfc-eng"
          git config user.email "58239972+nfc-eng@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Stage only the data directory
          git add data/

          # Only commit if there are changes
          if git diff --staged --quiet; then
            echo "No changes to commit (newsletter for today already exists)"
            echo "pushed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            TODAY=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
            git commit -m "Newsletter: ${TODAY}"
            git push origin HEAD
            echo "Newsletter committed and pushed for ${TODAY}"
            echo "pushed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Trigger Vercel deploy
        if: steps.commit.outputs.pushed == 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 2
        run: |
          curl -s --max-time 60 -X POST "https://api.vercel.com/v1/integrations/deploy/prj_1hmwUaJ1XIKqaCIK8t3G7Dbh1lfS/dKLuRFPKSN"
          echo "Vercel deploy hook triggered"

content.text:
All pushed to mainline. Here's where things stand:

**Done:**
- `generate-podcast.js` — Claude writes the script, ElevenLabs converts to MP3, uploads to Vercel Blob, patches the URL back into the newsletter JSON
- `AudioPlayer.tsx` — play/pause, scrubber, time display, styled to match the site
- `NewsletterView.tsx` — player appears below the editor's note when `audioUrl` is present
- GitHub Actions workflow — podcast runs automatically after each newsletter generation

**Still need from you:**
- The **Vercel Blob token** (`BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN`) added as a GitHub secret — this is the last missing piece before tomorrow's scheduled run will generate a podcast automatically

Once you add that secret, tomorrow at 6am it will generate the newsletter + podcast end to end with no intervention needed.

content.text:
All three secrets are in place:

- ✅ `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
- ✅ `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`
- ✅ `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN`

**One thing remaining** — install `@vercel/blob` in the repo. The package is in `package.json` but hasn't been `npm install`'d yet, so the `package-lock.json` doesn't have it. GitHub Actions runs `npm ci` which requires the lock file to be up to date.

Do you have the repo on your machine? If so run:

```bash
cd ~/AI-Daily-Flash && npm install && git add package-lock.json && git commit -m "Add @vercel/blob dependency" && git push origin mainline
```

Or I can do it — just need permission to run `npm install` in that directory.

  "requires": true,

content.text: No modern Node available locally — everything is v9/v12. The lock file got corrupted to v1 format. Let me restore the original lock file and switch the workflow to `npm install` instead of `npm ci` so GitHub Actions handles installing `@vercel/blob` itself:

input.command: git checkout HEAD -- package-lock.json && head -3 package-lock.json

content.content: "lockfileVersion": 3,

toolUseResult.stdout: "lockfileVersion": 3,

content.text: Good, v3 restored. Now update the workflow to use `npm install` so it picks up the new dependency automatically:

input.old_string:
- name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
input.new_string:
- name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Generate AI Newsletter

on:
  schedule:
    # 6 AM Eastern Standard Time (UTC-5) = 11:00 UTC
    # Note: shifts to 7 AM Eastern during summer (EDT, UTC-4)
    - cron: '0 11 * * *'

  # Allow manual trigger from the GitHub Actions tab
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      date_override:
        description: 'Override date (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave blank for today.'
        required: false
        default: ''

jobs:
  generate:
    name: Generate Newsletter
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 45
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: mainline
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Generate newsletter
        timeout-minutes: 35
        run: node scripts/generate-newsletter.js
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

      - name: Commit and push newsletter
        id: commit
        timeout-minutes: 5
        run: |
          git config user.name "nfc-eng"
          git config user.email "58239972+nfc-eng@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Stage only the data directory
          git add data/

          # Only commit if there are changes
          if git diff --staged --quiet; then
            echo "No changes to commit (newsletter for today already exists)"
            echo "pushed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            TODAY=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
            git commit -m "Newsletter: ${TODAY}"
            git push origin HEAD
            echo "Newsletter committed and pushed for ${TODAY}"
            echo "pushed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Generate podcast
        if: steps.commit.outputs.pushed == 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 15
        run: node scripts/generate-podcast.js
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          ELEVENLABS_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ELEVENLABS_API_KEY }}
          BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN }}

      - name: Commit podcast audio URL
        if: steps.commit.outputs.pushed == 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 5
        run: |
          git config user.name "nfc-eng"
          git config user.email "58239972+nfc-eng@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add data/
          if git diff --staged --quiet; then
            echo "No podcast changes to commit"
          else
            TODAY=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
            git commit -m "Podcast: ${TODAY}"
            git push origin HEAD
            echo "Podcast data committed for ${TODAY}"
          fi

      - name: Trigger Vercel deploy
        if: steps.commit.outputs.pushed == 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 2
        run: |
          curl -s --max-time 60 -X POST "https://api.vercel.com/v1/integrations/deploy/prj_1hmwUaJ1XIKqaCIK8t3G7Dbh1lfS/dKLuRFPKSN"
          echo "Vercel deploy hook triggered"

content.text:
<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user (Nicholas Clement, Sr. SDE at AWS) wanted to:
   - Set up ~/.claude as a version-controlled repo synced to github.com/nfc-eng/claude-config
   - Add anti-hallucination instructions via a global CLAUDE.md
   - Configure bash read-only permissions in settings.json
   - Work on the AI-Daily-Flash newsletter project (cloned to ~/AI-Daily-Flash)
   - Fix feed quality issues: remove GitHub .atom feeds causing Ghostty commit noise
   - Add new RSS feeds and tighten the editorial prompt
   - Add a daily podcast feature: Claude-written script → ElevenLabs TTS → Vercel Blob → audio player on site
   - Ensure GitHub Actions always runs off mainline branch
   - Keep nfc-eng/claude-config in sync whenever ~/.claude files change

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Claude Code global CLAUDE.md (auto-loaded every session from ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md)
   - GitHub CLI (gh) installed as binary to ~/bin/gh (Xcode too old for brew build)
   - Git version control of ~/.claude config folder → nfc-eng/claude-config repo
   - settings.json permission allowlist for read-only bash commands
   - RSS feed pipeline: rss-parser → Claude Opus 4.6 → structured JSON newsletter
   - GitHub Actions cron workflow (07:00 UTC = 6am EST) on mainline branch
   - ElevenLabs TTS API (voice ID: bIHbv24MWmeRgasZH58o "Will", model: eleven_turbo_v2_5)
   - Vercel Blob storage for MP3 hosting (@vercel/blob package)
   - Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript
   - Podcast script structure: cold open → intro → headlines → deep dive → outro (~1,400-1,600 words, ~10 min)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `/Users/nicholas/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (created)
     - Global anti-hallucination instructions loaded every session
     - Rules: admit uncertainty, ground in evidence, verify before recommending, restrict to provided context, retract unsupported claims, show reasoning

   - `/Users/nicholas/.claude/settings.json` (created/modified)
     - Read-only bash allowlist: ls, cat, grep, find, git status/log/diff/branch/show, jq, curl -s, brew list, npm list, etc.
     - Added: `"Edit(/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/*)"` and `"Write(/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/*)"` to allow edits without prompting

   - `/Users/nicholas/.claude/.gitignore` (created)
     - Excludes: plugins/marketplaces/, cache/, settings.local.json, *.env, *token*, *secret*, *credential*

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/scripts/generate-newsletter.js` (modified)
     - Removed 5 GitHub .atom feeds: Karpathy, Simon Willison, Marc Brooker, Mitchell Hashimoto, Boris Cherny
     - Added practitioner feeds: Emad Mostaque (emad.posthaven.com/rss), Nathan Benaich (nathanbenaich.substack.com/feed)
     - Added broader sources: Ben's Bites, SemiAnalysis, Weights & Biases, Allen AI, Roboflow, IBM Research
     - Tightened prompt: stronger filtering against non-AI stories, funding news, low-signal content; explicit rule to skip non-AI topics even from notable practitioners

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/scripts/generate-podcast.js` (created)
     - Full podcast pipeline: loads today's newsletter JSON → Claude generates script → ElevenLabs TTS → Vercel Blob upload → patches audioUrl into newsletter JSON
     - Saves script to data/scripts/YYYY-MM-DD.txt for debugging
     - Skips if audioUrl already exists in JSON
     - Requires: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/src/components/AudioPlayer.tsx` (created)
     - Client component with play/pause, scrubber, time display
     - Styled to match dark zinc aesthetic with cyan-500 play button
     - Uses HTML5 audio element with React state for currentTime/duration/loading/playing

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/src/components/NewsletterView.tsx` (modified)
     - Added `import AudioPlayer from '@/components/AudioPlayer'`
     - Added `{newsletter.audioUrl && <AudioPlayer audioUrl={newsletter.audioUrl} />}` below editor's note

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/src/lib/newsletter-types.ts` (modified)
     - Added `audioUrl?: string` to Newsletter interface

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/package.json` (modified)
     - Added `"@vercel/blob": "^0.27.0"` to dependencies
     - Added `"generate:podcast": "node scripts/generate-podcast.js"` script

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/.github/workflows/generate-newsletter.yml` (modified)
     - Added `ref: mainline` to checkout step to pin execution to mainline branch
     - Changed `npm ci` to `npm install` (local Node v12 corrupted lockfile to v1 format; npm install handles missing @vercel/blob automatically on Node 20 runner)
     - Added podcast generation step after newsletter commit:
       ```yaml
       - name: Generate podcast
         if: steps.commit.outputs.pushed == 'true'
         timeout-minutes: 15
         run: node scripts/generate-podcast.js
         env:
           ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
           ELEVENLABS_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ELEVENLABS_API_KEY }}
           BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN }}
       - name: Commit podcast audio URL
         ...commits data/ changes including audioUrl in newsletter JSON
       ```
     - Vercel deploy trigger remains last step

   - `/Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash/ROADMAP.md` (modified)
     - Added full podcast feature plan as top item in "Up Next" with 5-step implementation plan
     - Added Node.js Actions deprecation tech debt item (bump before June 2, 2026)
     - Resolved merge conflict: kept both user's thumbs up/save for later ideas and feature branch additions

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **gh CLI install via brew failed**: Xcode 11.3.1 too old, needs 14.2. Fixed by downloading pre-built amd64 binary directly to ~/bin/gh.
   - **Wrong architecture binary**: First downloaded arm64 binary, got "Bad CPU type" error. Fixed by checking `uname -m` (x86_64) and downloading amd64 version.
   - **git push rejected on mainline**: Remote had newer commits. Fixed with `git pull origin mainline --rebase && git push`.
   - **Merge conflict in ROADMAP.md**: When merging feature branch to mainline, ROADMAP had conflicting additions. Resolved by keeping both sides' content.
   - **package-lock.json corrupted to v1**: Local npm v6 / Node v12 downgraded lockfile format when running npm install. Fixed by: restoring original v3 lockfile via `git checkout HEAD -- package-lock.json`, then changing workflow from `npm ci` to `npm install` so the Node 20 GitHub Actions runner handles @vercel/blob installation.
   - **User shared API key in chat**: User pasted ElevenLabs API key directly in chat. Warned user to revoke immediately and add as GitHub secret instead.
   - **Commit message with single quotes**: `git commit -m` with heredoc containing apostrophes caused shell parse error. Fixed by using inline `-m "..."` string.
   - **Workflow running on wrong branch**: GitHub Actions schedule runs on default branch which was set to old feature branch. Fixed by adding `ref: mainline` to checkout step, and user changed default branch to mainline in GitHub settings.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified root cause of Ghostty commit noise: GitHub .atom feeds exposing meaningless commit activity. Removed all 5 GitHub atom feeds entirely rather than trying to filter in the prompt.
   - Solved lockfile version mismatch by restoring original and switching to `npm install` in CI — pragmatic fix that works correctly on the Node 20 runner without needing local Node upgrade.
   - Podcast pipeline designed to be fully automated and idempotent (skips if audioUrl already present).

6. All user messages:
   - "Can you improve yourself or can we make a new skill that gets loaded on every session that helps reduce hallucinations based on this? [URL]"
   - "Yes, but I would like to version control my dot clod root folder. Is that a good idea?"
   - "That is my repo, are you able to use that?" [github.com/nfc-eng/claude-config]
   - "no how can i set this up wiht my github"
   - "can you do it for me"
   - "please do it"
   - "should be good to go now"
   - "how can I let you edit all files in this repo?"
   - "run /update-config and say 'allow Edit and Write for /Users/nicholas/AI-Daily-Flash'"
   - "now did you push this to our claoude config"
   - "do i need to reload you now or"
   - "[/usage command] Please go ahead and clone this in a new repo. And then get familiar with it." [AI-Daily-Flash]
   - "so can we edit this to fetch more websites? and also it still is talking about random commits to ghosty..."
   - "i want to add https://strandsagents.com/blog/ to our list of resources please"
   - "ehhh nah lets save that for another day. how are we looking what changes do we have"
   - "what other feeds do we have in that .json file that are missing"
   - "yes please do all this and then push to mainline"
   - "how can I update my clog configuration such that you have bash read only permissions by default"
   - "Now can you make sure that our GitHub action always functions off mainline and not some other random branch"
   - "Okay I made mainline the default branch. Now how can we test if everything still works."
   - "did you commit the road map and did you pull to see if our last generation worked"
   - "add to roadmap [Node.js deprecation note] and then tell me what you need from me to make this podcast thing work"
   - "I would lprefer one host, yeah 8-12 minutes is perfect, maybe deep dive one article?"
   - "okay it is setup now, you should be able to write the code to create the podcast. go ahead on your side but first tell me what i need to do with vercel to get the bloc setup"
   - "api key is sk_42a6864163782d3e194f032dc8b3e5d41c3d7e5928cdb4be" [warned to revoke]
   - "env var or new repo secret"
   - "okay it is setup now" [re: secrets]
   - "ou do it for me. you know where we are working out of"
   - "okay I think that is added for vercel, what remains?"
   - "You silly boy. Can you also update this to make sure that in our knowledge that we always push changes we make to our cloud config repo?"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - None explicitly pending — all requested features are implemented and pushed to mainline
   - Tomorrow's 6am scheduled run will be the first full end-to-end test of the podcast pipeline
   - Optional: manually trigger a test workflow run to verify podcast generation works before the scheduled run

8. Current Work:
   The last task completed was fixing the npm lockfile issue. Local machine has Node v12/npm v6 which downgraded the package-lock.json from lockfileVersion 3 to 1 when running `npm install` to add @vercel/blob. Fixed by:
   1. Restoring the original v3 lockfile: `git checkout HEAD -- package-lock.json`
   2. Changing `npm ci` to `npm install` in the GitHub Actions workflow
   3. Committing and pushing to mainline

   All podcast feature code is now on mainline:
   - `scripts/generate-podcast.js` — full pipeline
   - `src/components/AudioPlayer.tsx` — player UI
   - `src/components/NewsletterView.tsx` — player wired in below editor's note
   - `src/lib/newsletter-types.ts` — audioUrl field added
   - `package.json` — @vercel/blob dependency added
   - `.github/workflows/generate-newsletter.yml` — podcast step added

   All three GitHub secrets confirmed present: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The user asked "want to manually trigger a test run now to see it all work end to end?" — this was the last suggested action and remains the logical next step. Trigger the workflow manually via:
   `~/bin/gh workflow run generate-newsletter.yml --repo nfc-eng/AI-Daily-Flash`
   This will run the full pipeline (newsletter + podcast) on mainline and verify everything works before tomorrow's scheduled run.
</summary>
